_comment: Expected KPI ranges for cuVSLAM nightly eval drift detection.
These are static, human-maintained guardrails, distinct from the rolling
nightly baseline in KPI_HISTORY (which changes every night).
cuvslam_kpi_report.py reads this via -b/--baseline_ranges and prints a SOFT
drift check (PASS/WITHIN/DRIFT) plus a <kpi>.json.drift report. It never fails
the job. Keys match the KPI JSON: <DATASET>_<METRIC>_<TYPE>_<ODOM|SLAM>.
Each entry has an 'expected' value and exactly one tolerance: 'tol_pct'
(percent of expected) or 'tol_abs' (absolute). expected=null means the KPI is
uncalibrated and is SKIPPED. To calibrate, copy the steady-state values from a
trusted nightly kpi_<date>.json into 'expected' below and pick a tolerance.
